body:
Title: CRA Form T1032 2025 Joint Election to Split Pension Income.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t1032/t1032-25e.txt

CRA T1032 2025 Step 2 calculates the maximum split-pension amount. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 131483.45, 12, 0.50, 2000, and 4000. Line 1 uses eligible pension income from line 8 of the transferring spouse's Federal Worksheet for line 31400. Line 2 uses T4A box 128. Line 3 is line 1 minus line 2. Line 4 uses T4A-RCA box 17 life annuity payments. Line 5 is 131,483.45. Line 7 is line 5 minus line 6, floored at zero. Line 8 is the lesser of line 4 and line 7. Lines 9 to 16 calculate eligible veterans benefits: line 12 adds line 10 and line 11, line 13 is 131,483.45, line 15 subtracts line 14 from line 13 and floors at zero, and line 16 is the lesser of line 9 and line 15. Line 17 adds lines 3, 8, and 16. Line 18 prorates line 17 by months married or common-law divided by months in the tax year, normally 12. Line 19 uses line 18 if proration applies, otherwise line 17. Line 20 is the maximum rate 50%, encoded as 0.50. Line 21 multiplies line 19 by line 20. Line 22 is the elected split-pension amount and cannot be more than line 21; it is entered on line 21000 of the transferring spouse's return and line 11600 of the receiving spouse's return.

CRA T1032 2025 Step 4 calculates pension income amount adjustments. For the transferring spouse, line 23 is line 1. If there is an amount on line 8, lines 24 to 30 allocate the elected amount: line 27 is line 25 divided by line 26, line 29 is line 27 multiplied by line 28, and line 30 is line 24 minus line 29. If there is no amount on line 8, line 30 is line 22. Line 31 is line 23 minus line 30. The transferor enters the lesser of $2,000 and line 31 on line 31400. For the receiving spouse, line 32 is the receiving spouse's Federal Worksheet line 8 amount if they have line 11500 or 12900 income. Line 33 normally uses line 30, subject to the note 1 special calculation for under-65 receiving spouses. Note 1 uses a $4,000 threshold; if the result after subtracting specified transferor payments is $4,000 or more, line 33 is line 30, otherwise line 33 is the lesser of line 22 and line 30. Line 34 adds lines 32 and 33. The receiving spouse enters the lesser of $2,000 and line 34 on line 31400.

CRA T1032 2025 Step 5 calculates income tax deducted. Line 35 uses total tax deducted from the transferring spouse's pension income entered on line 17. Line 36 is line 35 multiplied by line 22 divided by line 17. Line 37 is total tax deducted from all transferor slips before the split. Line 39 subtracts line 38 from line 37 and is entered on line 43700 of the transferor's return. Line 40 is total tax deducted from all receiving-spouse slips before the split. Line 42 adds line 41 to line 40 and is entered on line 43700 of the receiving spouse's return.
